     The Numbers Business Report

Get Rich or Die Tryin' (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $30,981,850Details
International Box Office $15,685,105
Worldwide Box Office $46,666,955
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $10,307,204
Total $10,307,204
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After losing his mother to murder as a boy, Marcus has little support in his life. He starts working for drug dealer Levar and his assistant AK. Although Marcus makes good money selling, his dream is to become a rapper. When he reunites with an old love and is later shot numerous times during a robbery, Marcus decides it’s time to change his life. However, his old associates won't let him go so easily.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$12,020,807 (38.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.58 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:66.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$40,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:1,652 opening theaters/1,666 max. theaters, 3.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $57,799,965

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 9th, 2005 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
Video Release: March 28th, 2006 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for strong violence, pervasive language, drug content, sexuality and nudity.
Running Time: 117 minutes
Plot point: Gangs, Non-Chronological
Social setting: Hood Film, Movie Business
Subgenre: Crime Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: MTV Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

After losing his mother to murder as a boy, Marcus has little support in his life. He starts working for drug dealer Levar and his assistant AK. Although Marcus makes good money selling, his dream is to become a rapper. When he reunites with an old love and is later shot numerous times during a robbery, Marcus decides it’s time to change his life. However, his old associates won't let him go so easily.
